title: Myonima parviflora
taxonomy: Myonima parviflora is classified within the family Salicaceae. However, some sources may group it under the family Flacourtiaceae, which is now considered a synonym for Salicaceae. Further classification places Myonima parviflora within the order Malpighiales. This order includes various flowering plants, some of which are commonly known as willows or spindles. At the rank of kingdom, Myonima parviflora is classified as a plant within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Myonima parviflora, also known as the "African bitter bush," is a plant species native to Southern Africa, belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. Traditionally, Myonima parviflora has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Myonima parviflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Myonima parviflora is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a tincture or decoction, to harness its medicinal benefits.
